events.label: reasoning
events.text: The scenario describes multiple serious hazards on an active jobsite: cords across walkways, missing guardrails, blocked egress/extinguishers, improvised scaffolds, unattended open holes. This is a safety & rigging housekeeping/hazard recognition task. Work is in-progress. Multiple OSHA violations. Decision should be fail, risk critical or high.

events.label: response
events.text:
{
  "decision": "fail",

  "findings": [
    "Electrical cords run across walkways creating trip and electrical hazards",
    "Missing guardrails at elevated/open-edge locations creating fall hazards",
    "Blocked egress paths preventing safe emergency exit",
    "Blocked or inaccessible fire extinguishers delaying emergency response",
    "Improvised/non-engineered scaffold in use without proper assembly or inspection",
    "Unattended open holes/floor penetrations without covers or barricades"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Stop affected work immediately and re-route/cord-protect or elevate cords out of walkways",
    "Install compliant guardrails or temporary fall protection before any elevated work continues",
    "Clear egress paths and keep exits unobstructed at all times",
    "Remove obstructions from fire extinguishers and verify access and signage",
    "Remove improvised scaffold; erect, inspect, and tag a compliant scaffold per 1926 Subpart L",
    "Cover and secure open holes with rated covers or barricade and post warnings"
  ],
  "rationale": "Multiple serious, concurrent hazards are present on an active jobsite, including fall hazards, trip/electrical hazards, blocked emergency egress and extinguishers, an improvised scaffold, and unguarded open holes. These violate OSHA general duty and 1926 Subpart L requirements and present imminent risk of serious injury or death; work cannot continue until controls are in place.",
  "references": [
    "OSHA General Duty Clause, 29 USC 654(a)(1)",
    "29 CFR 1926 Subpart L - Scaffolds",
    "29 CFR 1926.501 - Fall protection",
    "29 CFR 1910.37 - Exit routes maintenance",
    "29 CFR 1910.157 - Portable fire extinguishers access"
  ]
}
